annotations.note: "the passion, thoughtfulness and skill of the artists, writers and programmers" praises the dev team's effort/craftsmanship broadly but names no concrete game property or conduct charge (no promises/communication/support claim) -- treated as a diffuse quality-verdict (zero mentions), but flagging in case this should route to developer_conduct or split by discipline (art/writing).

mentions.evidence: impossible to put down — I've sunk hundreds of hours into it and still find myself coming back
The gameplay itself is genuinely brilliant
turning what used to be a thriving community into a cash-grab storefront
The toxicity in lobbies is real too, though the quick chat 'Nice shot!' after an own goal somehow never gets old.
it's gotten better over the years and since the last DLC and patch
Just when I was done playing this game they release a new update

annotations.note: Tone is ambiguous: could read as mildly positive (still actively supported / hard to fully quit) or mildly negative (mild exasperation); no explicit polarity word present, so scored neutral -- human should confirm.

mentions.evidence: This game will keep me occupied until Eu4
